learning_outcomes.description: Identify and explain the main conservation policy instruments and frameworks at international and national levels, and how they interact.
Articulate the policy development process and identify constraints on development and implementation of conservation policy
For a given conservation problem, identify alternative policy responses, and analyze their respective strengths and weaknesses.
Prepare credible, concise and evidence-informed policy positions/briefs on conservation issues, considering its socio-political context and other relevant issues.
Analyse the effectiveness of a policy, and identify and articulate ways to improve policy effectiveness within a set of real-world constraints.
Place common policy debates held in the public arena within their policy frameworks and identify motivations of stakeholders and interest groups in those debates.

assessments.task_description:
Following the Samuel review of the EPBC Act in 2020, there have been several proposed government policy responses. Several of these have been at least partly initiated, including changes to biodiversity offsetting, a new Nature Repair Market, and the foundations of new bioregional planning approaches. For Assignment 2, you will prepare a full review of ONE of these policy initiatives. For this Assignment 1, you will prepare an outline of this full review, including a timeline of key policy developments and an annotated bibliography of key resources. Feedback received will support the development of your full review in Assignment 2.

For Assignment 1, select one of the three Australian Federal policy initiatives about which the course staff will provide information and some key documentation: either bioregional planning, the Nature Repair Market, or biodiversity offsetting. Identify the policy’s objectives and the mechanism though which it seeks to achieve them, and research this type of policy instrument as well as the progression of the policy from at least the Samuel Review to the current day. Prepare a plan for your Assignment 2 that includes:

·      An outline of the sections that your Assignment 2 will include

·      A one-paragraph description of the policy and its objectives

·      A draft timeline in figure format, covering at least the period 2020-2026, indicating key milestones in the policy development process

·      An annotated bibliography of at least ten key resources that you will draw on for your policy review. These should include peer-reviewed literature, relevant government documents, and sources of stakeholder perspectives.

Submit via Turnitin by the due date a written report of maximum 1000 words, including the annotated bibliography, but not including text within the timeline figure.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. Refer to Blackboard for the submission link. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Following the Samuel review of the EPBC Act in 2020, there have been several proposed government policy responses. Several of these have been at least partly initiated, including changes to biodiversity offsetting, a new Nature Repair Market, and the foundations of new bioregional planning approaches. For Assignment 2, prepare a full review of ONE of these policy initiatives. You may include revised (if necessary) versions of the text and timeline included in Assignment 1.

For Assignment 2, select one of the three Australian Federal policy initiatives about which the course staff will provide information and some key documentation: either bioregional planning, the Nature Repair Market, or biodiversity offsetting.

Prepare a report that includes:

·      A one-paragraph description of the policy and its objectives.

·      An explanation of the type of policy instrument or instruments it employs, and a short critical review of these instrument types drawing on peer-reviewed and other literature.

·      A timeline in figure format, covering at least the period 2020-2026, indicating key milestones in the development of this specific policy in Australia

·      A summary of public critique, stakeholder perspectives and concerns regarding this policy during its development.

·      A well-reasoned analysis of the major risks and potential benefits of the policy as currently formulated, requirements for it to be effective, and recommendations for any changes, improvements, or safeguards.

Submit via Turnitin by the due date a written report of maximum 2500 words, not including the reference list or text within the timeline figure.

assessments.task_description:
Identify a conservation challenge and a policy response that you believe would help to address it. It can be from any jurisdiction in the world, but it must be a different issue that the one you examined in Assignments 1 and 2.

Imagine you are a policy advisor to the government Minister who would be primarily responsible for enacting this policy. Your role is to inform the Minister about the issue and proposed policy response in the form of a policy brief and a short spoken pitch, during which the Minister (your tutor) will ask you questions.

Prepare:

1) a succinct one-page policy brief using the template provided (with references),

2) a 300-word proposal for how the effectiveness of this policy would be evaluated after five years of its operation; and

3) a convincing persuasive spoken overview of the brief of one minute only.

Submit the written component as one document via Turnitin by the due date. Your spoken briefing will be presented in class in weeks 12 and 13. You will be graded on the written component, the persuasiveness and clarity of the spoken component, and your ability to respond effectively to the questions asked by your tutor.

In addition to submitting the written component, you must attend tutorials in Weeks 12 AND 13, and be ready to give your presentation and response to questions when called upon (unless alternative arrangements approved in advance)

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
